data.text: I'll start by reading the relevant files to understand the current schema and resolver structure.

input.description: Explore GraphQL schema and resolver structure
input.prompt:
I need to understand the GraphQL schema and resolver structure in this DDx project. Please find and read:

1. The main schema.graphql file (likely in cli/internal/server/ or similar)
2. Any existing resolver files for GraphQL
3. The FEAT-021 spec at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md
4. The exec store structure at .ddx/exec/ or .ddx/executions/
5. Any existing Run-related types in the schema

Search breadth: thorough. Report file paths and key content for each.

data.output: cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql
No files found
No files found
No files found
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md

1	# DDx GraphQL Schema
2	# Single source of truth for the SvelteKit UI.
3	# Every type field is doc-commented. Relay cursor connections on all lists.
4	
5	# ============================================
6	# Scalars
7	# ============================================
8	
9	"""ISO-8601 datetime string"""
10	scalar DateTime
11	
12	"""Arbitrary JSON blob (used for opaque map fields)"""
13	scalar JSON
14	
15	# ============================================
16	# Interfaces
17	# ============================================
18	
19	"""Node is the Relay global object identification interface"""
20	interface Node {
21	  """Globally unique identifier"""
22	  id: ID!
23	}
24	
25	# ============================================
26	# Pagination (Relay Cursor Connections)
27	# ============================================
28	
29	"""PageInfo carries cursor-based pagination state (Relay spec)"""
30	type PageInfo {
31	  """True when more edges exist after the last returned edge"""
32	  hasNextPage: Boolean!
33	  """True when more edges exist before the first returned edge"""
34	  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
35	  """Cursor of the first edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
36	  startCursor: String
37	  """Cursor of the last edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
38	  endCursor: String
39	}
40	
41	# ============================================
42	# Node & Project
43	# ============================================
44	
45	"""NodeInfo represents the running ddx-server instance"""
46	type NodeInfo implements Node {
47	  """Unique node identifier (stable across restarts)"""
48	  id: ID!
49	  """Human-readable node name"""
50	  name: String!
51	  """Timestamp when the server process started"""
52	  startedAt: DateTime!
53	  """Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat"""
54	  lastSeen: DateTime!
55	}
56	
57	"""Project represents a registered ddx project on this node"""
58	type Project implements Node {
59	  """Unique project identifier"""
60	  id: ID!
61	  """Short display name"""
62	  name: String!
63	  """Absolute filesystem path to the project root"""
64	  path: String!
65	  """Git remote URL, if configured"""
66	  gitRemote: String
67	  """When the project was first registered with the server"""
68	  registeredAt: DateTime!
69	  """Timestamp of the last successful probe"""
70	  lastSeen: DateTime!
71	  """True when the project path is inaccessible"""
72	  unreachable: Boolean
73	  """Set when the project has been tombstoned (soft-deleted)"""
74	  tombstonedAt: DateTime
75	}
76	
77	"""ProjectEdge is one edge in a ProjectConnection"""
78	type ProjectEdge {
79	  """The project at this position"""
80	  node: Project!
81	  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
82	  cursor: String!
83	}
84	
85	"""ProjectConnection is a Relay cursor connection of projects"""
86	type ProjectConnection {
87	  """Ordered list of project edges"""
88	  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
89	  """Pagination metadata"""
90	  pageInfo: PageInfo!
91	  """Total number of projects matching the filter"""
92	  totalCount: Int!
93	}
94	
95	# ============================================
96	# Beads
97	# ============================================
98	
99	"""Bead is a portable work item with lifecycle metadata"""
100	type Bead implements Node {
101	  """Unique bead identifier (e.g. "ddx-a1b2c3d4")"""
102	  id: ID!
103	  """Short summary of the work"""
104	  title: String!
105	  """Lifecycle status: open, in-progress, closed, blocked"""
106	  status: String!
107	  """Numeric priority (lower is higher priority)"""
108	  priority: Int!
109	  """Issue classification (e.g. "task", "bug", "spike")"""
110	  issueType: String!
111	  """Agent or human currently responsible"""
112	  owner: String
113	  """When the bead was created"""
114	  createdAt: DateTime!
115	  """Who or what created the bead"""
116	  createdBy: String
117	  """Last modification timestamp"""
118	  updatedAt: DateTime!
119	  """Freeform labels for filtering and grouping"""
120	  labels: [String!]
121	  """Project identifier this bead belongs to"""
122	  projectID: String
123	  """Parent bead ID for hierarchy"""
124	  parent: String
125	  """Full description / body text"""
126	  description: String
127	  """Acceptance criteria text"""
128	  acceptance: String
129	  """Freeform notes appended during work"""
130	  notes: String
131	  """Dependency edges to other beads"""
132	  dependencies: [Dependency!]
133	}
134	
135	"""Dependency is a directed edge between two beads"""
136	type Dependency {
137	  """The bead that has the dependency"""
138	  issueId: String!
139	  """The bead that must be completed first"""
140	  dependsOnId: String!
141	  """Dependency kind (e.g. "blocks", "relates-to")"""
142	  type: String!
143	  """When the dependency was created"""
144	  createdAt: String
145	  """Who created the dependency"""
146	  createdBy: String
147	  """Opaque metadata attached to the link"""
148	  metadata: String
149	}
150	
151	"""BeadEdge is one edge in a BeadConnection"""
152	type BeadEdge {
153	  """The bead at this position"""
154	  node: Bead!
155	  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
156	  cursor: String!
157	}
158	
159	"""BeadConnection is a Relay cursor connection of beads"""
160	type BeadConnection {
161	  """Ordered list of bead edges"""
162	  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
163	  """Pagination metadata"""
164	  pageInfo: PageInfo!
165	  """Total number of beads matching the filter"""
166	  totalCount: Int!
167	}
168	
169	"""BeadStatusCounts holds aggregate bead counts by status"""
170	type BeadStatusCounts {
171	  """Number of beads in open status"""
172	  open: Int!
173	  """Number of beads in closed status"""
174	  closed: Int!
175	  """Number of beads with unclosed dependencies"""
176	  blocked: Int!
177	  """Number of open beads whose dependencies are all closed"""
178	  ready: Int!
179	  """Total number of beads"""
180	  total: Int!
181	}
182	
183	# ============================================
184	# Documents (library)
185	# ============================================
186	
187	"""Document represents a docgraph document with dependency tracking"""
188	type Document implements Node {
189	  """Document identifier (usually the relative path)"""
190	  id: ID!
191	  """Filesystem path relative to the library root"""
192	  path: String!
193	  """Human-readable title extracted from frontmatter"""
194	  title: String!
195	  """IDs of documents this document depends on"""
196	  dependsOn: [String!]!
197	  """IDs of input artifacts consumed by this document"""
198	  inputs: [String!]!
199	  """IDs of documents that depend on this document"""
200	  dependents: [String!]!
201	  """Review state metadata (null if never reviewed)"""
202	  review: DocumentReview
203	  """True if parked (excluded from staleness checks)"""
204	  parkingLot: Boolean!
205	  """Prompt text embedded in the document frontmatter"""
206	  prompt: String
207	  """Execution definition embedded in the document (null if none)"""
208	  execDef: DocumentExecDef
209	  """Raw file content (populated only by documentByPath, null in list queries)"""
210	  content: String
211	}
212	
213	"""DocumentReview holds review-state metadata for a document"""
214	type DocumentReview {
215	  """Hash of the document content at review time"""
216	  selfHash: String!
217	  """Map of dependency IDs to their hashes at review time"""
218	  deps: JSON!
219	  """ISO-8601 timestamp of the last review"""
220	  reviewedAt: String!
221	}
222	
223	"""DocumentExecDef holds an execution definition embedded in a document"""
224	type DocumentExecDef {
225	  """Definition identifier"""
226	  id: String
227	  """IDs of artifacts this definition produces or consumes"""
228	  artifactIds: [String!]
229	  """How to invoke the execution"""
230	  executor: ExecutorSpec
231	  """How to interpret the result"""
232	  result: ResultSpec
233	  """Evaluation criteria and thresholds"""
234	  evaluation: Evaluation
235	  """Whether the definition is currently active"""
236	  active: Boolean
237	  """Whether the definition must pass for merge"""
238	  required: Boolean
239	  """Whether this definition was discovered from the docgraph"""
240	  graphSource: Boolean
241	  """When the definition was created"""
242	  createdAt: DateTime
243	}
244	
245	"""ExecutorSpec describes how to invoke an execution"""
246	type ExecutorSpec {
247	  """Execution kind (e.g. "shell", "agent")"""
248	  kind: String!
249	  """Command and arguments to run"""
250	  command: [String!]
251	  """Working directory for the command"""
252	  cwd: String
253	  """Environment variables as key-value pairs"""
254	  env: JSON
255	  """Maximum execution time in milliseconds"""
256	  timeoutMs: Int
257	}
258	
259	"""ResultSpec describes how to interpret an execution result"""
260	type ResultSpec {
261	  """Metric-shaped projection (null if not metric-typed)"""
262	  metric: MetricResultSpec
263	}
264	
265	"""MetricResultSpec defines metric extraction from execution output"""
266	type MetricResultSpec {
267	  """Unit of measurement (e.g. "ms", "bytes")"""
268	  unit: String
269	  """JSONPath to the numeric value"""
270	  valuePath: String
271	  """JSONPath to the samples array"""
272	  samplesPath: String
273	}
274	
275	"""Evaluation holds interpretation rules for execution results"""
276	type Evaluation {
277	  """Comparison operator (e.g. "lt", "gt", "eq")"""
278	  comparison: String
279	  """Threshold values for warn/ratchet"""
280	  thresholds: Thresholds
281	}
282	
283	"""Thresholds captures the ratchet policy for metric evaluations"""
284	type Thresholds {
285	  """Warn threshold in milliseconds"""
286	  warnMs: Float
287	  """Ratchet threshold in milliseconds"""
288	  ratchetMs: Float
289	}
290	
291	"""DocumentEdge is one edge in a DocumentConnection"""
292	type DocumentEdge {
293	  """The document at this position"""
294	  node: Document!
295	  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
296	  cursor: String!
297	}
298	
299	"""DocumentConnection is a Relay cursor connection of documents"""
300	type DocumentConnection {
301	  """Ordered list of document edges"""
302	  edges: [DocumentEdge!]!
303	  """Pagination metadata"""
304	  pageInfo: PageInfo!
305	  """Total number of documents matching the filter"""
306	  totalCount: Int!
307	}
308	
309	# ============================================
310	# Doc Graph
311	# ============================================
312	
313	"""DocGraph represents the full document dependency graph"""
314	type DocGraph {
315	  """Absolute path to the docgraph root directory"""
316	  rootDir: String!
317	  """All documents in the graph"""
318	  documents: [Document!]!
319	  """Map of relative paths to document IDs"""
320	  pathToId: JSON!
321	  """Map of document IDs to lists of dependent IDs"""
322	  dependents: JSON!
323	  """Warnings generated during graph construction. Derived from issues; prefer the structured issues field when rendering UI."""
324	  warnings: [String!]!
325	  """Structured integrity issues for the graph, grouped by kind on the client."""
326	  issues: [GraphIssue!]!
327	}
328	
329	"""GraphIssue describes one integrity problem discovered while building the document graph."""
330	type GraphIssue {
331	  """Machine-readable category (e.g. duplicate_id, missing_dep)."""
332	  kind: String!
333	  """Path of the offending document, when known."""
334	  path: String
335	  """Document identifier associated with the issue, when known."""
336	  id: String
337	  """Human-readable explanation suitable for the UI or terminal."""
338	  message: String!
339	  """Related document path (e.g. the pre-existing path a duplicate ID collides with)."""
340	  relatedPath: String
341	}
342	
343	"""StaleReason explains why a specific document is stale"""
344	type StaleReason {
345	  """Document identifier"""
346	  id: ID!
347	  """Filesystem path of the stale document"""
348	  path: String!
349	  """Human-readable explanations of why the document is stale"""
350	  reasons: [String!]!
351	}
352	
353	# ============================================
354	# Search
355	# ============================================
356	
357	"""SearchResult represents a single search hit from the library"""
358	type SearchResult {
359	  """Filesystem path of the matching resource"""
360	  path: String!
361	  """Resource type (e.g. "prompt", "template", "persona")"""
362	  type: String!
363	  """Display name of the resource"""
364	  name: String!
365	  """Excerpt showing the match context"""
366	  snippet: String
367	}
368	
369	"""SearchResultEdge is one edge in a SearchResultConnection"""
370	type SearchResultEdge {
371	  """The search result at this position"""
372	  node: SearchResult!
373	  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
374	  cursor: String!
375	}
376	
377	"""SearchResultConnection is a Relay cursor connection of search results"""
378	type SearchResultConnection {
379	  """Ordered list of search result edges"""
380	  edges: [SearchResultEdge!]!
381	  """Pagination metadata"""
382	  pageInfo: PageInfo!
383	  """Total number of results matching the query"""

10	# Feature: Multi-Node Dashboard UI
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-021
13	**Status:** In Progress
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	Extends the FEAT-008 web UI with a node/project-aware dashboard. The UI can
20	show a combined view of beads and DDx run activity across all registered
21	projects on a node, or narrow to a specific project for context-dependent
22	views (artifact browser, dependency graph, run history, commit log). Node,
23	project, and page are all embedded in the URL so every view is bookmarkable
24	and shareable.
25	
26	The dashboard UI is implemented as a SvelteKit application with graphql-request for
27	GraphQL data fetching. Every page uses `+page.ts` load functions to fetch data
28	from the `/graphql` endpoint defined in SD-022.
29	
30	## Problem Statement
31	
32	**Current situation:** The FEAT-008 UI is implicitly scoped to the single
33	project the server was started in. There is no node concept, no project picker,
34	and no combined cross-project view for beads or layer-aware run history.
35	
36	**Pain points:**
37	- Operator cannot see all active work across projects in one dashboard
38	- Navigating to a different project requires restarting the server in that
39	  project's directory
40	- Deep links to a specific bead, artifact, or run record are not stable because the URL
41	  carries no project context
42	- Queue-drain evidence is flattened as agent sessions instead of preserving
43	  the `work` -> `try` -> `run` hierarchy from FEAT-010
44	- Node identity is invisible in the UI — there is no indication of which
45	  machine or server instance is being viewed
46	
47	**Desired outcome:** A single `ddx server` UI that surfaces everything
48	happening on the node. The operator can scan the full bead queue and run
49	history across all projects, drill into a specific project for artifact-level
50	work, and bookmark any view with a stable URL.
51	
52	## URL Structure
53	
54	All routes embed context so every view is directly addressable:
55	
56	```
57	/                                     → redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
58	/nodes/:nodeId                        → node overview (health, project list)
59	/nodes/:nodeId/runs                   → combined run history (all projects)
60	/nodes/:nodeId/beads                  → combined bead queue (all projects)
61	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId                   → project overview
62	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads             → project beads
63	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts         → artifact browser
64	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents         → document browser
65	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph             → doc dependency graph
66	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits           → commit log
67	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs              → project run history
68	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId       → project run detail
69	```
70	
71	**Combined views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/runs`, `/nodes/:nodeId/beads`) operate
72	across all registered projects. They call node-scoped read APIs for beads and
73	the unified run substrate (since one server = one node). A future multi-node
74	coordinator would fan these out across nodes.
75	
76	**Project-scoped views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...`) pass the
77	project ID to the existing `/api/projects/:project/...` API routes defined in
78	SD-019. The UI must always resolve which project context to bind before
79	making API calls.
80	
81	The `:nodeId` segment is the stable node ID from `GET /api/node` (e.g.
82	`node-7029e8d6`). The `:projectId` segment is the stable project ID from
83	`GET /api/projects` (e.g. `proj-96d7ea83`).
84	
85	## Views
86	
87	### Node Overview (`/nodes/:nodeId`)
88	
89	- Node name, ID, and uptime
90	- Health summary cards: library status, bead store, doc graph per project
91	- Project list with last-seen timestamp and health badge
92	- Quick links to combined runs and combined beads
93	- Server version and started_at
94	
95	**GraphQL Query:** `node` query from FEAT-020, `projects` query for project list.
96	
97	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/+page.svelte`
98	
99	### Combined Bead Queue (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`)
100	
101	All beads from all registered projects merged into one view. The project each
102	bead belongs to is shown as a column/badge. Supports all filter/sort/search
103	capabilities from FEAT-008 US-082, applied across projects.
104	
105	- Project filter chip to narrow to one project (updates URL)
106	- Full-text search across all projects (client-side)
107	- Status columns: open, in_progress, closed
108	- Priority sort within each column
109	- Clicking a bead navigates to its project-scoped detail:
110	  `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads#:beadId`
111	
112	**GraphQL Query:** `beads` with `projectID:` argument per SD-019.
113	
114	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/beads/+page.svelte`
115	
116	### Combined Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/runs`)
117	
118	All DDx run records across all registered projects, newest first. The project
119	each record belongs to is shown inline, and records retain their FEAT-010 layer
120	label: `work`, `try`, or `run`.
121	
122	- Project filter to narrow
123	- Harness filter
124	- Layer filter (`work`, `try`, `run`)
125	- Status filter
126	- Time range filter
127	- Click to expand: DDx metadata, native session references, token usage,
128	  parent/child links, and artifact producer links
129	- Same capabilities as FEAT-008 US-086 and US-086b
130	
131	**GraphQL Query:** `runs` from FEAT-010 with optional `projectID` omitted.
132	
133	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/runs/+page.svelte`
134	
135	### Project Overview (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`)
136	
137	- Project name, path, git remote
138	- Library configured: yes/no
139	- Bead summary: counts by status
140	- Recent run activity: last 5 run records
141	- Quick links to project-scoped views
142	
143	**GraphQL Query:** `project` by ID, `beads` count aggregation, `runs`
144	filtered by project.
145	
146	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte`
147	
148	### Project Beads (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads`)
149	
150	Same as FEAT-008 bead views (list, kanban, ready queue, detail) scoped to one
151	project. This is the same UI already specified in FEAT-008 US-082 through
152	US-086, with the URL carrying the project context.
153	
154	**GraphQL Query:** `beadsByProject` with cursor pagination per SD-019.
155	
156	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+page.svelte`
157	
158	### Artifact Browser (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts`)
159	
160	FEAT-008 artifact browser scoped to the selected project.
161	
162	**GraphQL Query:** `artifacts` with project context per SD-019.
163	
164	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte`
165	
166	### Document Browser (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents`)
167	
168	Compatibility route for markdown-focused document navigation. It redirects to
169	or aliases the project artifact browser with a `media_type=text/markdown`
170	filter; new navigation should use `/artifacts`.
171	
172	**GraphQL Query:** `artifacts` with project context per SD-019.
173	
174	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/documents/+page.svelte`
175	
176	### Document Dependency Graph (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph`)
177	
178	FEAT-008 dependency graph view scoped to the selected project.
179	
180	**GraphQL Query:** `docGraph` per SD-019.
181	
182	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte`
183	
184	### Commit Log (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits`)
185	
186	Git commit history for the project, served by a new `GET /api/projects/:project/commits`
187	endpoint. Displays:
188	- Commit SHA (short), author, date, subject line
189	- Expand to show full commit message and changed files
190	- Filter by author, date range
191	- Link from a commit to any bead whose `closing_commit_sha` matches
192	
193	**GraphQL Query:** `commits` with cursor pagination per SD-019.
194	
195	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/commits/+page.svelte`
196	
197	### Project Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs`)
198	
199	DDx run records filtered to one project. This is the same UI as the combined
200	run history view with the project context bound in the URL.
201	
202	**GraphQL Query:** `runs` with project filter per FEAT-010.
203	
204	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte`
205	
206	### Project Run Detail (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId`)
207	
208	Layer-aware run detail for a single `work`, `try`, or `run` record. Work
209	records show child try attempts, try records show child layer-1 runs, and
210	layer-1 runs show prompt/config summary, selected harness/provider/model,
211	power bounds, cost/token/duration signals, evidence links, and produced
212	artifact links.
213	
214	**GraphQL Query:** `run` by id with project filter per FEAT-010.
215	
216	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte`
217	
218	## Navigation
219	
220	The global navigation bar shows:
221	- Node name + ID (links to node overview)
222	- Project picker dropdown (populated from `/api/projects`)
223	  - Selecting a project navigates to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`
224	  - "All projects" option navigates to combined views
225	- Active page tab: Beads | Artifacts | Graph | Runs | Commits
226	
227	The project picker changes the `:projectId` segment in-place while preserving
228	the current page tab. So switching project while on the Graph tab navigates to
229	the new project's graph directly. When switching from a run detail to another
230	project, the UI falls back to that project's run history unless the same run id
231	exists in the target project.
232	
233	**GraphQL Query:** `projects` for picker options.
234	
235	**File:** `src/lib/components/ProjectPicker.svelte`
236	
237	## Requirements
238	
239	### Functional
240	
241	1. Root `/` redirects to `/nodes/:nodeId` using the ID from `GET /api/node`.
242	2. All URLs listed in the URL Structure section are valid deep links — loading
243	   them directly in a browser renders the correct view with data.
244	3. The node overview page is populated from `GET /api/node` and
245	   `GET /api/projects`.
246	4. Combined bead view fetches all projects' beads client-side and merges them.
247	5. Combined run view fetches node-wide run records from the unified run
248	   substrate and preserves `work`, `try`, and `run` layer labels.
249	6. Project-scoped views bind all API calls to
250	   `/api/projects/:project/...` per SD-019.
251	7. The project picker is present on every project-scoped page and updates the
252	   URL when changed.
253	8. A new `GET /api/projects/:project/commits` endpoint serves git commit
254	   history for the project.
255	9. Commit log view links commits to matching beads where
256	   `closing_commit_sha` matches.
257	10. All filter/sort/search state is stored in URL query parameters so filtered
258	    views are also bookmarkable.
259	11. Project run detail routes support parent/child drill-down:
260	    `work` -> `try` -> `run`, with breadcrumbs back up the hierarchy.
261	12. Project artifact routes expose FEAT-008 media-type rendering and link to
262	    producing runs when `generated_by` is present.
263	
264	### Non-Functional
265	
266	- Combined bead view with 500 beads across 5 projects renders in <500ms
267	  (client-side merge).
268	- Navigation between views within the same node+project uses client-side
269	  routing (no full page reload).
270	- The UI degrades gracefully when a project becomes unavailable: its beads and
271	  run records are shown with a stale badge rather than being silently dropped.
272	
273	## User Stories
274	
275	### US-090: Operator Views All Active Work in One Dashboard
276	**As an** operator managing multiple projects on one machine
277	**I want** to see all open beads and recent run records from every project
278	**So that** I can understand the full workload without switching context
279	
280	**Acceptance Criteria:**
281	- Given multiple projects are registered, when I open `/nodes/:nodeId/beads`,
282	  then I see beads from all projects with a project badge on each
283	- Given I filter by project, then only beads from that project are shown and
284	  the URL updates to reflect the filter
285	- Given I open `/nodes/:nodeId/runs`, then I see run records from all projects
286	  merged and sorted by time with a project badge and a layer badge (`work`,
287	  `try`, or `run`) on each record
288	- Given I click a run record, then I navigate to that run's project-scoped
289	  detail at `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId`; the `projectId`
290	  is carried in each run record so the combined list can form this URL without
291	  a separate lookup; I see layer-appropriate detail: queue inputs for `work`,
292	  bead/worktree fields for `try`, execution summary for `run`
293	- Given I navigate back from a run detail, then the combined run list is
294	  restored with the same sort and filter state
295	
296	**E2E Test:** `node-beads.spec.ts` and `node-runs.spec.ts` — full workflows:
297	- node-beads: open combined beads → verify cross-project beads with project badges → filter by project → verify URL and filtered results → click bead → verify navigation to project-scoped bead detail → press Back
298	- node-runs: open combined runs → verify cross-project records with layer and project badges → filter by layer → click work record → verify detail → click child try → verify fields → press Back through hierarchy
299	
300	### US-091: Operator Navigates to a Project-Scoped View
301	**As an** operator investigating one project
302	**I want** to select a project and see its artifacts, graph, runs, and commits
303	**So that** I can do project-specific work without losing the node context
304	
305	**Acceptance Criteria:**
306	- Given I am on the node overview, when I click a project, then I navigate to
307	  that project's overview at `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`
308	- Given I am on the artifact browser for project A, when I switch projects in
309	  the picker, then I navigate to the artifact browser for project B without
310	  losing the current page context (e.g. still on `/artifacts`)
311	- Given I copy the current URL and open it in a new tab, then I see the same
312	  view with the same project and page selected
313	- Given I switch projects while on a run detail, then the UI falls back to
314	  that project's run history
315	
316	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: open node overview → click project → verify project overview URL → open artifact browser → switch project in picker → verify URL and content updated to project B → copy URL → open in new tab → verify same view → navigate to run detail → switch project → verify fallback to run history
317	
318	### US-092: Operator Bookmarks a Filtered View
319	**As an** operator who checks the ready queue every morning
320	**I want** to bookmark the ready queue filtered to my project
321	**So that** I can open it directly without navigating through the UI
322	
323	**Acceptance Criteria:**
324	- Given I filter the bead list to status=open and project=ddx, then the URL
325	  contains those filter parameters
326	- Given I reload the page with that URL, then the same filter is applied
327	  without any interaction
328	- Given I share the URL with a colleague on the same machine, then they see
329	  the same view
330	
331	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: apply status + project filters → verify URL params → copy URL → reload page → verify same filter applied without interaction
332	
333	### US-093: Operator Traces a Bead to Its Closing Commit
334	**As an** operator reviewing completed work
335	**I want** to see the git commit that closed a bead
336	**So that** I can inspect the code change that fulfilled the bead's acceptance
337	
338	**Acceptance Criteria:**
339	- Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when I view the commit log, then
340	  that commit is highlighted and linked to the bead
341	- Given I click the commit link from the bead detail, then I navigate to the
342	  commit log with that commit in view
343	- Given I click the bead link from the commit row, then I navigate to that
344	  bead's detail
345	
346	**E2E Test:** `commits.spec.ts` — full workflow: open bead with closing commit → click commit link → verify commit log opens with commit highlighted → click bead link from commit row → verify bead detail opens → press Back → verify commit log restored at same position
347	
348	### US-094: Operator Sees Node Identity in the UI
349	**As an** operator with multiple machines
350	**I want** to see which node I am connected to
351	**So that** I don't confuse the eitri server with the bragi server
352	
353	**Acceptance Criteria:**
354	- Given I open the web UI, then the node name and ID are visible in the
355	  navigation bar or node overview
356	- Given the URL contains `:nodeId`, then it matches the ID returned by
357	  `GET /api/node`
358	
359	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: open UI → verify node name and ID visible in nav → verify URL nodeId matches GET /api/node response
360	
361	### US-094b: Operator Opens Layer-Aware Run Routes
362	**As an** operator reviewing DDx execution evidence
363	**I want** project-scoped run routes for history and detail
364	**So that** queue drains, bead attempts, and single agent invocations remain
365	  distinct in the URL and UI
366	
367	**Acceptance Criteria:**
368	- Given I navigate to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs`, then I see
369	  project-scoped run records with filter chips for layer, status, bead,
370	  harness, and time range; active filters are encoded in the URL
371	- Given I click a `work` record, then I navigate to
372	  `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId` and see the work detail:
373	  queue inputs, selected beads, stop condition, and child try attempts
374	- Given I click a child `try`, then I see bead id, base/result revisions,
375	  worktree path, merge/preserve result, checks, and child layer-1 runs
376	- Given I click a layer-1 `run`, then I see prompt/config summary, power
377	  bounds, selected harness/provider/model, tokens, cost, duration, output, and
378	  evidence links
379	- Given I am viewing any run detail, then breadcrumbs show the full
380	  `work → try → run` path and each crumb is a navigable link
381	- Given a run produced an artifact, then the detail route links to the artifact
382	  browser entry and that artifact links back to the producing run
383	
384	**E2E Test:** `runs.spec.ts` — full workflow: navigate to project runs → apply layer filter → verify URL → click work record → verify work detail fields → click child try → verify try fields → click layer-1 run → verify all fields → follow evidence link → press Back through breadcrumbs to run list → verify filter state restored → follow artifact link from run → verify artifact links back to run
385	
386	## New API Required
387	
388	In addition to the endpoints delivered by FEAT-020 and FEAT-010, FEAT-021
389	requires:
390	
391	| Endpoint | Description |
392	|----------|-------------|
393	| `GET /api/projects/:project/commits` | Git log for the project; supports `?limit=&since=&author=` |
394	
395	All other data is served by endpoints already defined in FEAT-002, FEAT-004,
396	FEAT-006, FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and SD-019.
397	
398	## Implementation Notes
399	
400	### Router
401	
402	SvelteKit handles routing automatically based on `src/routes/` file structure.
403	Routes are nested using the folder hierarchy:
404	
405	```
406	src/routes/
407	├── +layout.svelte              → Node+Project layout with nav
408	├── +page.ts                    → Root redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
409	└── nodes/
410	    └── [$nodeId]/
411	        ├── +layout.ts          → Load node context
412	        ├── +page.svelte        → NodeOverview
413	        ├── beads/              → CombinedBeads
414	        │   └── +page.svelte
415	        ├── runs/               → CombinedRuns
416	        │   └── +page.svelte
417	        └── projects/
418	            └── [$projectId]/
419	                ├── +page.svelte      → ProjectOverview
420	                ├── beads/            → ProjectBeads
421	                │   └── +page.svelte
422	                ├── artifacts/        → ArtifactBrowser
423	                │   └── +page.svelte
424	                ├── documents/        → DocumentBrowser
425	                │   └── +page.svelte
426	                ├── graph/            → DocGraph
427	                │   └── +page.svelte
428	                ├── commits/          → CommitLog
429	                │   └── +page.svelte
430	                └── runs/             → ProjectRuns
431	                    ├── +page.svelte
432	                    └── [$runId]/
433	                        └── +page.svelte
434	```
435	
436	The `[$nodeId]` and `[$projectId]` dynamic route segments capture URL parameters.
437	SvelteKit's `+layout.ts` and `+page.ts` files define load functions that
438	fetch data via graphql-request.
439	
440	### Data Layer
441	
442	graphql-request is the GraphQL client for data fetching, with graphql-ws for
443	subscriptions. Pages define queries inline using the `gql` tagged template
444	and manually typed response interfaces in `+page.ts` load functions.
445	
446	Example bead list query:
447	
448	```typescript
449	// src/routes/nodes/$nodeId/beads/+page.ts
450	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';
451	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
452	
453	const BEAD_LIST_QUERY = gql`
454	  query BeadList($projectID: ID) {
455	    beads(projectID: $projectID, first: 50) {
456	      edges {
457	        node {
458	          id
459	          title
460	          status
461	          priority
462	          projectID
463	        }
464	      }
465	    }
466	  }
467	`;
468	
469	export const load = async ({ fetch, params }) => {
470	  const client = createClient(fetch);
471	  const data = await client.request(BEAD_LIST_QUERY, { projectID: params.projectId });
472	  return { beads: data.beads };
473	};
474	```
475	
476	graphql-ws subscription support enables real-time updates without polling.
477	
478	Example project run query:
479	
480	```typescript
481	// src/routes/nodes/$nodeId/projects/$projectId/runs/+page.ts
482	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';
483	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
484	
485	const RUN_LIST_QUERY = gql`
486	  query ProjectRuns($projectID: ID!, $layer: RunLayer) {
487	    runs(projectID: $projectID, layer: $layer, first: 50) {
488	      edges {
489	        node {
490	          id
491	          layer
492	          status
493	          beadID
494	          artifactID
495	          parentRunID
496	          startedAt
497	          completedAt
498	        }
499	      }
500	    }
501	  }
502	`;
503	
504	export const load = async ({ fetch, params, url }) => {
505	  const client = createClient(fetch);
506	  const layer = url.searchParams.get('layer');
507	  const data = await client.request(RUN_LIST_QUERY, {
508	    projectID: params.projectId,
509	    layer
510	  });
511	  return { runs: data.runs };
512	};
513	```
514	
515	### Project Context
516	
517	The `ProjectStore` (using Svelte 5 runes) stores the selected node ID and
518	project ID. It's populated from URL params by `+layout.ts` load functions:
519	
520	```typescript
521	// src/lib/stores/project.ts
522	import { writable } from 'svelte/store';
523	
524	export const projectStore = writable<{
525	  nodeId: string;
526	  projectId?: string;
527	} | null>(null);
528	```
529	
530	Components read from `projectStore` to construct API URLs and filter queries.
531	
532	### Testing
533	
534	Playwright e2e tests verify the complete end-to-end workflow for each user
535	story. Each spec covers the full flow: entry point → actions → outcome →
536	navigation back. Tests use real ddx-server with seeded fixture data; no
537	mocking of the GraphQL or HTTP layer.
538	
539	| E2E Spec | User Stories | Workflow Covered |
540	|---|---|---|
541	| `navigation.spec.ts` | US-091, US-092, US-094 | Root redirect → node overview → project picker → project switch preserving page context → bookmarkable URL round-trip → node identity visible |
542	| `node-beads.spec.ts` | US-090 (beads) | Combined bead view → cross-project badges → filter by project → click bead → navigate to project-scoped detail → Back |
543	| `node-runs.spec.ts` | US-090 (runs) | Combined run history → cross-project layer badges → filter → drill work→try→run → breadcrumbs back to list |
544	| `runs.spec.ts` | US-094b | Project runs → apply layer filter → work detail → try detail → run detail → evidence link → artifact link → Back through full hierarchy |
545	| `commits.spec.ts` | US-093 | Bead with closing commit → commit log → highlighted commit → bead cross-link → Back |
546	
547	## Dependencies
548	
549	- FEAT-008 (web UI foundation — build pipeline, embed pattern, existing views)
550	- FEAT-020 (node identity and project registry — required for /api/node and /api/projects)
551	- SD-019 (project-scoped API routing — /api/projects/:project/...)
552	- FEAT-004 (beads)
553	- FEAT-006 (agent service metadata and native session references)
554	- FEAT-007 (doc graph)
555	- FEAT-010 (unified run substrate and run read APIs)
556	- FEAT-012 (git awareness — for commit log endpoint)
557	
558	## Out of Scope
559	
560	- Multi-node federation (coordinator aggregating state from multiple servers)
561	- Cross-machine views (each node's UI shows only what that node knows)
562	- Authentication
563	- Real-time push (polling is sufficient for v1)
564	- Mobile layout
565

11	// NewResolver constructs a Resolver with mandatory fields validated.
12	// Returns an error if state is nil or workingDir is empty, ensuring callers

14	func NewResolver(state StateProvider, workingDir string) (*Resolver, error) {

## Summary: GraphQL Schema and Resolver Structure in DDx Project

I've completed a thorough analysis of the GraphQL schema, resolver structure, and execution storage system in this DDx project. Here are the key findings:

### 1. GraphQL Schema Location
**File:** `/cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql` (2795 lines)

The schema is a comprehensive GraphQL interface using:
- Relay cursor connections for pagination on all list queries
- Node interface for global object identification
- Scalar types: `DateTime` (ISO-8601), `JSON` (opaque blobs)

### 2. Execution-Related Types in Schema

**Execution (Execute-Bead Bundle)** (lines 937-994)
- Represents one complete execute-bead attempt bundle stored at `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/`
- Joins bead, session, prompt, verdict, and tool-call stream together
- Fields include: id (stable = bundle dir name), projectId, beadId, sessionId, harness, model, verdict, status, rationale, createdAt, startedAt, finishedAt, durationMs, costUsd, tokens, baseRev, resultRev, bundlePath, prompt, manifest (raw JSON), result (raw JSON)

**ExecutionRun** (lines 821-857)
- Represents one invocation of an ExecutionDefinition (checks, benchmarks, etc.)
- Fields: id, definitionId, artifactIds, startedAt, finishedAt, status, exitCode, mergeBlocking, agentSessionId, attachments, provenance, metric, stdout, stderr, value, unit, parsed

**ExecutionDefinition** (lines 781-801)
- Describes a repeatable execution (check, benchmark, etc.)
- Fields: id, artifactIds, executor (ExecutorSpec), result (ResultSpec), evaluation (Evaluation), active, required, graphSource, createdAt

**ExecutionToolCall** (lines 1014-1030)
- One tool_use/tool_result pair in an execution's agent-log stream
- Fields: id, name, seq, ts, inputs (JSON string), output (string, may be truncated), truncated boolean

**Supporting Types:**
- ExecutorSpec: How to invoke (kind, command, cwd, env, timeoutMs)
- ResultSpec: How to interpret (metric field)
- Provenance: Actor, host, gitRev, ddxVersion
- MetricObservation: artifactId, definitionId, observedAt, status, value, unit, samples, comparison
- ComparisonResult: baseline, delta, direction

### 3. Resolver Files and Interfaces

**Primary Resolver:** `/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_executions.go` (176 lines)

Implements `ExecutionsStateProvider` interface:
```go
type ExecutionsStateProvider interface {
    GetExecutionsGraphQL(projectID string, filter ExecutionFilter) []*Execution
    GetExecutionGraphQL(id string) (*Execution, bool)
    GetExecutionToolCallsGraphQL(id string) []*ExecutionToolCall
}
```

Key Query Resolvers:
- `Executions()` - List with filters (beadId, verdict, harness, since/until timestamps, search text)
- `Execution()` - Single execution detail
- `ExecutionBySessionID()` - Lookup by session
- `ExecutionByResultRev()` - Lookup by git commit SHA
- `ExecutionToolCalls()` - Paginated tool-call stream

**Subscription Resolver:** `/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_sub_exec.go` (159 lines)
- `ExecutionEvidence` - Polls log provider, streams stdout/stderr lines
- `CoordinatorMetrics` - Polls and emits coordinator metrics snapshots

### 4. Execution Store Structure (.ddx/executions/)

**Physical Structure:**
```
.ddx/executions/20260501T015153-0e17571b/
├── manifest.json      (request metadata: attempt_id, bead_id, base_rev, created_at, requested, bead, governing, paths)
├── result.json        (execution outcome: verdict, status, harness, session_id, duration_ms, tokens, cost_usd, exit_code)
├── prompt.md          (full prompt/instruction text)
├── usage.json         (token usage breakdown)
├── checks.json        (optional: execution checks)
└── [agent-log]        (optional: tool-call stream from agent)
```

**Bundle ID Format:** ISO8601 timestamp + 8-char hash (e.g., `20260501T015153-0e17571b`)

**Backend Loading:** `/cli/internal/server/state_executions.go` (150+ lines)
- Merges manifest.json + result.json into unified Execution record
- Handles both rich and terse schema variants
- Extracts metadata: bead info, harness/model, verdict, status, rationale, session correlations, git revisions
- Loads bundle files on-demand for detail views

### 5. Run-Related Types

**ExecutionRun** is the primary run type (distinct from Execution):
- Tied to ExecutionDefinition (e.g., a CI check definition)
- Captures execution outcome: status, exitCode, metrics, stdout/stderr
- Supports merge-blocking verdicts and metric observations

The schema does not currently have a generic "Run" type; layer-aware runs appear to be modeled in FEAT-010 (not visible in this search). ExecutionRun focuses on definition-driven runs (checks/benchmarks), while Execution focuses on execute-bead attempts.

### 6. FEAT-021 Requirements (Dashboard UI Spec)

**File:** `/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md` (565 lines)

Key Dashboard Views Requiring Execution Data:
- Combined Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/runs`) - All runs across projects with layer labels (work/try/run)
- Project Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs`) - Project-scoped runs
- Run Detail (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId`) - Layer-aware hierarchy (work → try → run)

URL Structure: All views embed `:nodeId` and `:projectId` for bookmarkable deep links.

### 7. Key Integration Points

**GraphQL Queries Available for Dashboard:**
- `executions(projectId, first, after, last, before, beadId, verdict, harness, since, until, search)` → ExecutionConnection
- `execution(id)` → Execution (full: prompt + manifest + result)
- `executionBySessionId(projectId, sessionId)` → Execution
- `executionByResultRev(projectId, sha)` → Execution
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` → ExecutionToolCallConnection

**Relay Pagination:** All list types use cursor-based pagination with hasNextPage/hasPreviousPage signals.

### File Paths Summary

1. **GraphQL Schema:** `/cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql`
2. **Execution Resolvers:** `/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_executions.go`
3. **Subscription Resolvers:** `/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_sub_exec.go`
4. **Backend State Loading:** `/cli/internal/server/state_executions.go`
5. **Test Integration:** `/cli/internal/server/graphql/executions_integration_test.go`
6. **Feature Spec:** `/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md`
7. **Sample Execution Bundle:** `.ddx/executions/20260501T015153-0e17571b/` (manifest.json, result.json, prompt.md, usage.json)

This provides a complete view of how executions are modeled, stored, and served via GraphQL to the dashboard UI.

9	---
10	# Feature: Multi-Node Dashboard UI
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-021
13	**Status:** In Progress
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	Extends the FEAT-008 web UI with a node/project-aware dashboard. The UI can
20	show a combined view of beads and DDx run activity across all registered
21	projects on a node, or narrow to a specific project for context-dependent
22	views (artifact browser, dependency graph, run history, commit log). Node,
23	project, and page are all embedded in the URL so every view is bookmarkable
24	and shareable.
25	
26	The dashboard UI is implemented as a SvelteKit application with graphql-request for
27	GraphQL data fetching. Every page uses `+page.ts` load functions to fetch data
28	from the `/graphql` endpoint defined in SD-022.
29	
30	## Problem Statement
31	
32	**Current situation:** The FEAT-008 UI is implicitly scoped to the single
33	project the server was started in. There is no node concept, no project picker,
34	and no combined cross-project view for beads or layer-aware run history.
35	
36	**Pain points:**
37	- Operator cannot see all active work across projects in one dashboard
38	- Navigating to a different project requires restarting the server in that
39	  project's directory
40	- Deep links to a specific bead, artifact, or run record are not stable because the URL
41	  carries no project context
42	- Queue-drain evidence is flattened as agent sessions instead of preserving
43	  the `work` -> `try` -> `run` hierarchy from FEAT-010
44	- Node identity is invisible in the UI — there is no indication of which
45	  machine or server instance is being viewed
46	
47	**Desired outcome:** A single `ddx server` UI that surfaces everything
48	happening on the node. The operator can scan the full bead queue and run
49	history across all projects, drill into a specific project for artifact-level
50	work, and bookmark any view with a stable URL.
51	
52	## URL Structure
53	
54	All routes embed context so every view is directly addressable:
55	
56	```
57	/                                     → redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
58	/nodes/:nodeId                        → node overview (health, project list)
59	/nodes/:nodeId/runs                   → combined run history (all projects)
60	/nodes/:nodeId/beads                  → combined bead queue (all projects)
61	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId                   → project overview
62	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads             → project beads
63	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts         → artifact browser
64	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents         → document browser
65	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph             → doc dependency graph
66	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits           → commit log
67	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs              → project run history
68	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId       → project run detail
69	```
70	
71	**Combined views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/runs`, `/nodes/:nodeId/beads`) operate
72	across all registered projects. They call node-scoped read APIs for beads and
73	the unified run substrate (since one server = one node). A future multi-node
74	coordinator would fan these out across nodes.
75	
76	**Project-scoped views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...`) pass the
77	project ID to the existing `/api/projects/:project/...` API routes defined in
78	SD-019. The UI must always resolve which project context to bind before
79	making API calls.
80	
81	The `:nodeId` segment is the stable node ID from `GET /api/node` (e.g.
82	`node-7029e8d6`). The `:projectId` segment is the stable project ID from
83	`GET /api/projects` (e.g. `proj-96d7ea83`).
84	
85	## Views
86	
87	### Node Overview (`/nodes/:nodeId`)
88	
89	- Node name, ID, and uptime
90	- Health summary cards: library status, bead store, doc graph per project
91	- Project list with last-seen timestamp and health badge
92	- Quick links to combined runs and combined beads
93	- Server version and started_at
94	
95	**GraphQL Query:** `node` query from FEAT-020, `projects` query for project list.
96	
97	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/+page.svelte`
98	
99	### Combined Bead Queue (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`)
100	
101	All beads from all registered projects merged into one view. The project each
102	bead belongs to is shown as a column/badge. Supports all filter/sort/search
103	capabilities from FEAT-008 US-082, applied across projects.
104	
105	- Project filter chip to narrow to one project (updates URL)
106	- Full-text search across all projects (client-side)
107	- Status columns: open, in_progress, closed
108	- Priority sort within each column
109	- Clicking a bead navigates to its project-scoped detail:
110	  `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads#:beadId`
111	
112	**GraphQL Query:** `beads` with `projectID:` argument per SD-019.
113	
114	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/beads/+page.svelte`
115	
116	### Combined Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/runs`)
117	
118	All DDx run records across all registered projects, newest first. The project
119	each record belongs to is shown inline, and records retain their FEAT-010 layer
120	label: `work`, `try`, or `run`.
121	
122	- Project filter to narrow
123	- Harness filter
124	- Layer filter (`work`, `try`, `run`)
125	- Status filter
126	- Time range filter
127	- Click to expand: DDx metadata, native session references, token usage,
128	  parent/child links, and artifact producer links
129	- Same capabilities as FEAT-008 US-086 and US-086b
130	
131	**GraphQL Query:** `runs` from FEAT-010 with optional `projectID` omitted.
132	
133	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/runs/+page.svelte`
134	
135	### Project Overview (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`)
136	
137	- Project name, path, git remote
138	- Library configured: yes/no
139	- Bead summary: counts by status
140	- Recent run activity: last 5 run records
141	- Quick links to project-scoped views
142	
143	**GraphQL Query:** `project` by ID, `beads` count aggregation, `runs`
144	filtered by project.
145	
146	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte`
147	
148	### Project Beads (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads`)
149	
150	Same as FEAT-008 bead views (list, kanban, ready queue, detail) scoped to one
151	project. This is the same UI already specified in FEAT-008 US-082 through
152	US-086, with the URL carrying the project context.
153	
154	**GraphQL Query:** `beadsByProject` with cursor pagination per SD-019.
155	
156	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+page.svelte`
157	
158	### Artifact Browser (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts`)
159	
160	FEAT-008 artifact browser scoped to the selected project.
161	
162	**GraphQL Query:** `artifacts` with project context per SD-019.
163	
164	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte`
165	
166	### Document Browser (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents`)
167	
168	Compatibility route for markdown-focused document navigation. It redirects to
169	or aliases the project artifact browser with a `media_type=text/markdown`
170	filter; new navigation should use `/artifacts`.
171	
172	**GraphQL Query:** `artifacts` with project context per SD-019.
173	
174	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/documents/+page.svelte`
175	
176	### Document Dependency Graph (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph`)
177	
178	FEAT-008 dependency graph view scoped to the selected project.
179	
180	**GraphQL Query:** `docGraph` per SD-019.
181	
182	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte`
183	
184	### Commit Log (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits`)
185	
186	Git commit history for the project, served by a new `GET /api/projects/:project/commits`
187	endpoint. Displays:
188	- Commit SHA (short), author, date, subject line
189	- Expand to show full commit message and changed files
190	- Filter by author, date range
191	- Link from a commit to any bead whose `closing_commit_sha` matches
192	
193	**GraphQL Query:** `commits` with cursor pagination per SD-019.
194	
195	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/commits/+page.svelte`
196	
197	### Project Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs`)
198	
199	DDx run records filtered to one project. This is the same UI as the combined
200	run history view with the project context bound in the URL.
201	
202	**GraphQL Query:** `runs` with project filter per FEAT-010.
203	
204	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte`
205	
206	### Project Run Detail (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId`)
207	
208	Layer-aware run detail for a single `work`, `try`, or `run` record. Work
209	records show child try attempts, try records show child layer-1 runs, and
210	layer-1 runs show prompt/config summary, selected harness/provider/model,
211	power bounds, cost/token/duration signals, evidence links, and produced
212	artifact links.
213	
214	**GraphQL Query:** `run` by id with project filter per FEAT-010.
215	
216	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte`
217	
218	## Navigation
219	
220	The global navigation bar shows:
221	- Node name + ID (links to node overview)
222	- Project picker dropdown (populated from `/api/projects`)
223	  - Selecting a project navigates to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`
224	  - "All projects" option navigates to combined views
225	- Active page tab: Beads | Artifacts | Graph | Runs | Commits
226	
227	The project picker changes the `:projectId` segment in-place while preserving
228	the current page tab. So switching project while on the Graph tab navigates to
229	the new project's graph directly. When switching from a run detail to another
230	project, the UI falls back to that project's run history unless the same run id
231	exists in the target project.
232	
233	**GraphQL Query:** `projects` for picker options.
234	
235	**File:** `src/lib/components/ProjectPicker.svelte`
236	
237	## Requirements
238	
239	### Functional
240	
241	1. Root `/` redirects to `/nodes/:nodeId` using the ID from `GET /api/node`.
242	2. All URLs listed in the URL Structure section are valid deep links — loading
243	   them directly in a browser renders the correct view with data.
244	3. The node overview page is populated from `GET /api/node` and
245	   `GET /api/projects`.
246	4. Combined bead view fetches all projects' beads client-side and merges them.
247	5. Combined run view fetches node-wide run records from the unified run
248	   substrate and preserves `work`, `try`, and `run` layer labels.
249	6. Project-scoped views bind all API calls to
250	   `/api/projects/:project/...` per SD-019.
251	7. The project picker is present on every project-scoped page and updates the
252	   URL when changed.
253	8. A new `GET /api/projects/:project/commits` endpoint serves git commit
254	   history for the project.
255	9. Commit log view links commits to matching beads where
256	   `closing_commit_sha` matches.
257	10. All filter/sort/search state is stored in URL query parameters so filtered
258	    views are also bookmarkable.
259	11. Project run detail routes support parent/child drill-down:
260	    `work` -> `try` -> `run`, with breadcrumbs back up the hierarchy.
261	12. Project artifact routes expose FEAT-008 media-type rendering and link to
262	    producing runs when `generated_by` is present.
263	
264	### Non-Functional
265	
266	- Combined bead view with 500 beads across 5 projects renders in <500ms
267	  (client-side merge).
268	- Navigation between views within the same node+project uses client-side
269	  routing (no full page reload).
270	- The UI degrades gracefully when a project becomes unavailable: its beads and
271	  run records are shown with a stale badge rather than being silently dropped.
272	
273	## User Stories
274	
275	### US-090: Operator Views All Active Work in One Dashboard
276	**As an** operator managing multiple projects on one machine
277	**I want** to see all open beads and recent run records from every project
278	**So that** I can understand the full workload without switching context
279	
280	**Acceptance Criteria:**
281	- Given multiple projects are registered, when I open `/nodes/:nodeId/beads`,
282	  then I see beads from all projects with a project badge on each
283	- Given I filter by project, then only beads from that project are shown and
284	  the URL updates to reflect the filter
285	- Given I open `/nodes/:nodeId/runs`, then I see run records from all projects
286	  merged and sorted by time with a project badge and a layer badge (`work`,
287	  `try`, or `run`) on each record
288	- Given I click a run record, then I navigate to that run's project-scoped
289	  detail at `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId`; the `projectId`
290	  is carried in each run record so the combined list can form this URL without
291	  a separate lookup; I see layer-appropriate detail: queue inputs for `work`,
292	  bead/worktree fields for `try`, execution summary for `run`
293	- Given I navigate back from a run detail, then the combined run list is
294	  restored with the same sort and filter state
295	
296	**E2E Test:** `node-beads.spec.ts` and `node-runs.spec.ts` — full workflows:
297	- node-beads: open combined beads → verify cross-project beads with project badges → filter by project → verify URL and filtered results → click bead → verify navigation to project-scoped bead detail → press Back
298	- node-runs: open combined runs → verify cross-project records with layer and project badges → filter by layer → click work record → verify detail → click child try → verify fields → press Back through hierarchy
299	
300	### US-091: Operator Navigates to a Project-Scoped View
301	**As an** operator investigating one project
302	**I want** to select a project and see its artifacts, graph, runs, and commits
303	**So that** I can do project-specific work without losing the node context
304	
305	**Acceptance Criteria:**
306	- Given I am on the node overview, when I click a project, then I navigate to
307	  that project's overview at `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`
308	- Given I am on the artifact browser for project A, when I switch projects in
309	  the picker, then I navigate to the artifact browser for project B without
310	  losing the current page context (e.g. still on `/artifacts`)
311	- Given I copy the current URL and open it in a new tab, then I see the same
312	  view with the same project and page selected
313	- Given I switch projects while on a run detail, then the UI falls back to
314	  that project's run history
315	
316	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: open node overview → click project → verify project overview URL → open artifact browser → switch project in picker → verify URL and content updated to project B → copy URL → open in new tab → verify same view → navigate to run detail → switch project → verify fallback to run history
317	
318	### US-092: Operator Bookmarks a Filtered View
319	**As an** operator who checks the ready queue every morning
320	**I want** to bookmark the ready queue filtered to my project
321	**So that** I can open it directly without navigating through the UI
322	
323	**Acceptance Criteria:**
324	- Given I filter the bead list to status=open and project=ddx, then the URL
325	  contains those filter parameters
326	- Given I reload the page with that URL, then the same filter is applied
327	  without any interaction
328	- Given I share the URL with a colleague on the same machine, then they see
329	  the same view
330	
331	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: apply status + project filters → verify URL params → copy URL → reload page → verify same filter applied without interaction
332	
333	### US-093: Operator Traces a Bead to Its Closing Commit
334	**As an** operator reviewing completed work
335	**I want** to see the git commit that closed a bead
336	**So that** I can inspect the code change that fulfilled the bead's acceptance
337	
338	**Acceptance Criteria:**
339	- Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when I view the commit log, then
340	  that commit is highlighted and linked to the bead
341	- Given I click the commit link from the bead detail, then I navigate to the
342	  commit log with that commit in view
343	- Given I click the bead link from the commit row, then I navigate to that
344	  bead's detail
345	
346	**E2E Test:** `commits.spec.ts` — full workflow: open bead with closing commit → click commit link → verify commit log opens with commit highlighted → click bead link from commit row → verify bead detail opens → press Back → verify commit log restored at same position
347	
348	### US-094: Operator Sees Node Identity in the UI
349	**As an** operator with multiple machines
350	**I want** to see which node I am connected to
351	**So that** I don't confuse the eitri server with the bragi server
352	
353	**Acceptance Criteria:**
354	- Given I open the web UI, then the node name and ID are visible in the
355	  navigation bar or node overview
356	- Given the URL contains `:nodeId`, then it matches the ID returned by
357	  `GET /api/node`
358	
359	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: open UI → verify node name and ID visible in nav → verify URL nodeId matches GET /api/node response
360	
361	### US-094b: Operator Opens Layer-Aware Run Routes
362	**As an** operator reviewing DDx execution evidence
363	**I want** project-scoped run routes for history and detail
364	**So that** queue drains, bead attempts, and single agent invocations remain
365	  distinct in the URL and UI
366	
367	**Acceptance Criteria:**
368	- Given I navigate to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs`, then I see
369	  project-scoped run records with filter chips for layer, status, bead,
370	  harness, and time range; active filters are encoded in the URL
371	- Given I click a `work` record, then I navigate to
372	  `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId` and see the work detail:
373	  queue inputs, selected beads, stop condition, and child try attempts
374	- Given I click a child `try`, then I see bead id, base/result revisions,
375	  worktree path, merge/preserve result, checks, and child layer-1 runs
376	- Given I click a layer-1 `run`, then I see prompt/config summary, power
377	  bounds, selected harness/provider/model, tokens, cost, duration, output, and
378	  evidence links
379	- Given I am viewing any run detail, then breadcrumbs show the full
380	  `work → try → run` path and each crumb is a navigable link
381	- Given a run produced an artifact, then the detail route links to the artifact
382	  browser entry and that artifact links back to the producing run
383	
384	**E2E Test:** `runs.spec.ts` — full workflow: navigate to project runs → apply layer filter → verify URL → click work record → verify work detail fields → click child try → verify try fields → click layer-1 run → verify all fields → follow evidence link → press Back through breadcrumbs to run list → verify filter state restored → follow artifact link from run → verify artifact links back to run
385	
386	## New API Required
387	
388	In addition to the endpoints delivered by FEAT-020 and FEAT-010, FEAT-021
389	requires:
390	
391	| Endpoint | Description |
392	|----------|-------------|
393	| `GET /api/projects/:project/commits` | Git log for the project; supports `?limit=&since=&author=` |
394	
395	All other data is served by endpoints already defined in FEAT-002, FEAT-004,
396	FEAT-006, FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and SD-019.
397	
398	## Implementation Notes
399	
400	### Router
401	
402	SvelteKit handles routing automatically based on `src/routes/` file structure.
403	Routes are nested using the folder hierarchy:
404	
405	```
406	src/routes/
407	├── +layout.svelte              → Node+Project layout with nav
408	├── +page.ts                    → Root redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
409	└── nodes/
410	    └── [$nodeId]/
411	        ├── +layout.ts          → Load node context
412	        ├── +page.svelte        → NodeOverview
413	        ├── beads/              → CombinedBeads
414	        │   └── +page.svelte
415	        ├── runs/               → CombinedRuns
416	        │   └── +page.svelte
417	        └── projects/
418	            └── [$projectId]/
419	                ├── +page.svelte      → ProjectOverview
420	                ├── beads/            → ProjectBeads
421	                │   └── +page.svelte
422	                ├── artifacts/        → ArtifactBrowser
423	                │   └── +page.svelte
424	                ├── documents/        → DocumentBrowser
425	                │   └── +page.svelte
426	                ├── graph/            → DocGraph
427	                │   └── +page.svelte
428	                ├── commits/          → CommitLog
429	                │   └── +page.svelte
430	                └── runs/             → ProjectRuns
431	                    ├── +page.svelte
432	                    └── [$runId]/
433	                        └── +page.svelte
434	```
435	
436	The `[$nodeId]` and `[$projectId]` dynamic route segments capture URL parameters.
437	SvelteKit's `+layout.ts` and `+page.ts` files define load functions that
438	fetch data via graphql-request.
439	
440	### Data Layer
441	
442	graphql-request is the GraphQL client for data fetching, with graphql-ws for
443	subscriptions. Pages define queries inline using the `gql` tagged template
444	and manually typed response interfaces in `+page.ts` load functions.
445	
446	Example bead list query:
447	
448	```typescript
449	// src/routes/nodes/$nodeId/beads/+page.ts
450	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';
451	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
452	
453	const BEAD_LIST_QUERY = gql`
454	  query BeadList($projectID: ID) {
455	    beads(projectID: $projectID, first: 50) {
456	      edges {
457	        node {
458	          id
459	          title
460	          status
461	          priority
462	          projectID
463	        }
464	      }
465	    }
466	  }
467	`;
468	
469	export const load = async ({ fetch, params }) => {
470	  const client = createClient(fetch);
471	  const data = await client.request(BEAD_LIST_QUERY, { projectID: params.projectId });
472	  return { beads: data.beads };
473	};
474	```
475	
476	graphql-ws subscription support enables real-time updates without polling.
477	
478	Example project run query:
479	
480	```typescript
481	// src/routes/nodes/$nodeId/projects/$projectId/runs/+page.ts
482	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';
483	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
484	
485	const RUN_LIST_QUERY = gql`
486	  query ProjectRuns($projectID: ID!, $layer: RunLayer) {
487	    runs(projectID: $projectID, layer: $layer, first: 50) {
488	      edges {
489	        node {
490	          id
491	          layer
492	          status
493	          beadID
494	          artifactID
495	          parentRunID
496	          startedAt
497	          completedAt
498	        }
499	      }
500	    }
501	  }
502	`;
503	
504	export const load = async ({ fetch, params, url }) => {
505	  const client = createClient(fetch);
506	  const layer = url.searchParams.get('layer');
507	  const data = await client.request(RUN_LIST_QUERY, {
508	    projectID: params.projectId,
509	    layer
510	  });
511	  return { runs: data.runs };
512	};
513	```
514	
515	### Project Context
516	
517	The `ProjectStore` (using Svelte 5 runes) stores the selected node ID and
518	project ID. It's populated from URL params by `+layout.ts` load functions:
519	
520	```typescript
521	// src/lib/stores/project.ts
522	import { writable } from 'svelte/store';
523	
524	export const projectStore = writable<{
525	  nodeId: string;
526	  projectId?: string;
527	} | null>(null);
528	```
529	
530	Components read from `projectStore` to construct API URLs and filter queries.
531	
532	### Testing
533	
534	Playwright e2e tests verify the complete end-to-end workflow for each user
535	story. Each spec covers the full flow: entry point → actions → outcome →
536	navigation back. Tests use real ddx-server with seeded fixture data; no
537	mocking of the GraphQL or HTTP layer.
538	
539	| E2E Spec | User Stories | Workflow Covered |
540	|---|---|---|
541	| `navigation.spec.ts` | US-091, US-092, US-094 | Root redirect → node overview → project picker → project switch preserving page context → bookmarkable URL round-trip → node identity visible |
542	| `node-beads.spec.ts` | US-090 (beads) | Combined bead view → cross-project badges → filter by project → click bead → navigate to project-scoped detail → Back |
543	| `node-runs.spec.ts` | US-090 (runs) | Combined run history → cross-project layer badges → filter → drill work→try→run → breadcrumbs back to list |
544	| `runs.spec.ts` | US-094b | Project runs → apply layer filter → work detail → try detail → run detail → evidence link → artifact link → Back through full hierarchy |
545	| `commits.spec.ts` | US-093 | Bead with closing commit → commit log → highlighted commit → bead cross-link → Back |
546	
547	## Dependencies
548	
549	- FEAT-008 (web UI foundation — build pipeline, embed pattern, existing views)
550	- FEAT-020 (node identity and project registry — required for /api/node and /api/projects)
551	- SD-019 (project-scoped API routing — /api/projects/:project/...)
552	- FEAT-004 (beads)
553	- FEAT-006 (agent service metadata and native session references)
554	- FEAT-007 (doc graph)
555	- FEAT-010 (unified run substrate and run read APIs)
556	- FEAT-012 (git awareness — for commit log endpoint)
557	
558	## Out of Scope
559	
560	- Multi-node federation (coordinator aggregating state from multiple servers)
561	- Cross-machine views (each node's UI shows only what that node knows)
562	- Authentication
563	- Real-time push (polling is sufficient for v1)
564	- Mobile layout
565

1990		ProviderKindEndpoint ProviderKind = "ENDPOINT"
1991		// Subprocess harness invoked via a local binary (e.g. claude, codex, gemini).
1992		ProviderKindHarness ProviderKind = "HARNESS"

1997		ProviderKindHarness,
1998	}

2000	func (e ProviderKind) IsValid() bool {
2001		switch e {
2002		case ProviderKindEndpoint, ProviderKindHarness:
2003			return true
2004		}

2006	}

2013		str, ok := v.(string)
2014		if !ok {
2015			return fmt.Errorf("enums must be strings")
2016		}
2017	
2018		*e = ProviderKind(str)
2019		if !e.IsValid() {
2020			return fmt.Errorf("%s is not a valid ProviderKind", str)
2021		}
2022		return nil
2023	}

2025	func (e ProviderKind) MarshalGQL(w io.Writer) {
2026		fmt.Fprint(w, strconv.Quote(e.String()))
2027	}
2028	
2029	func (e *ProviderKind) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error {
2030		s, err := strconv.Unquote(string(b))
2031		if err != nil {
2032			return err
2033		}
2034		return e.UnmarshalGQL(s)
2035	}
2036	
2037	func (e ProviderKind) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
2038		var buf bytes.Buffer

# ============================================
# Runs (FEAT-010 three-layer work/try/run substrate)
# ============================================

"""RunLayer identifies which layer of the work/try/run hierarchy a Run belongs to"""
enum RunLayer {
  """Top-level queue drain: encompasses one or more try attempts"""
  work
  """Single bead attempt: encompasses one or more run invocations"""
  try
  """Single agent invocation with a specific harness/model"""
  run
}

"""
Run is a record in the three-layer work→try→run substrate from the FEAT-010
agent execute workflow. It is distinct from ExecutionRun (definition-driven
check/benchmark results) and Execution (execute-bead bundle).
"""
type Run implements Node {
  """Stable identifier for this run record"""
  id: ID!
  """Which layer this record belongs to"""
  layer: RunLayer!
  """Current status (pending, running, success, failure, preserved)"""
  status: String!
  """When the run started"""
  startedAt: DateTime
  """When the run completed (null if still running)"""
  completedAt: DateTime
  """Bead ID associated with this run (null for work-layer records with multiple beads)"""
  beadId: String
  """Artifact ID produced by this run (null if none)"""
  artifactId: String
  """Parent run ID (null for top-level work records)"""
  parentRunId: String
  """IDs of child runs (try records for work, run records for try)"""
  childRunIds: [String!]!

  # ── Work-layer fields (populated when layer = work) ────────────────────
  """Raw queue inputs as JSON (work layer only)"""
  queueInputs: JSON
  """Stop condition for the queue drain (work layer only)"""
  stopCondition: String
  """Bead IDs selected for this queue drain (work layer only)"""
  selectedBeadIds: [String!]

  # ── Try-layer fields (populated when layer = try) ──────────────────────
  """Git revision the try attempt started from (try layer only)"""
  baseRevision: String
  """Git revision produced by the try attempt (try layer only)"""
  resultRevision: String
  """Isolated worktree path used during the attempt (try layer only)"""
  worktreePath: String
  """Merge or preserve outcome (try layer only)"""
  mergeOutcome: String
  """Check results as JSON (try layer only)"""
  checkResults: JSON

  # ── Run-layer fields (populated when layer = run) ──────────────────────
  """Short summary of the prompt (run layer only)"""
  promptSummary: String
  """Minimum power/effort level requested (run layer only)"""
  powerMin: Int
  """Maximum power/effort level requested (run layer only)"""
  powerMax: Int
  """Agent harness used (run layer only)"""
  harness: String
  """Provider name (run layer only)"""
  provider: String
  """Model identifier (run layer only)"""
  model: String
  """Input token count (run layer only)"""
  tokensIn: Int
  """Output token count (run layer only)"""
  tokensOut: Int
  """Estimated cost in USD (run layer only)"""
  costUsd: Float
  """Duration in milliseconds (run layer only)"""
  durationMs: Int
  """Short excerpt from the agent output (run layer only)"""
  outputExcerpt: String
  """Links to execution evidence (bundle paths, log paths, etc.) (run layer only)"""
  evidenceLinks: [String!]
}

"""RunEdge is one edge in a RunConnection"""
type RunEdge {
  """The run at this position"""
  node: Run!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""RunConnection is a Relay cursor connection of runs"""
type RunConnection {
  """Ordered list of run edges"""
  edges: [RunEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of runs matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Coordinators
# ============================================

    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti
...

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(graphql): add RunLayer enum + Run type + runs/run queries [ddx-37a24699]

Adds the FEAT-010 three-layer work/try/run substrate to the DDx GraphQL API:
- RunLayer enum and Run type with all layer-specific fields in schema.graphql
- runs(projectID, layer, beadID, status, harness, first, after): RunConnection! and run(id): Run? queries
- RunsStateProvider sub-interface + RunFilter + resolver implementations in resolver_runs.go
- state_runs.go: reads .ddx/exec/runs/ store and synthesizes run-layer records from .ddx/executions/ bundles
- Integration tests and unit filter tests in resolver_runs_test.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
